newsSince: No new company-specific catalyst since June 9. The relevant fundamentals predate the window: ~$735M of E-Pod data-center orders (first shipments early 2027) and 2026 EPS guidance of $5.29–$6.09 that management itself framed as a 'more measured/soft' approach. Stock +4% since June 9 and +52% YTD on data-center optimism, not fresh news.

sources.label: RRX soft guidance vs data-center orders (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental evidence since June 9. After +52% YTD and recurring bullish data-center coverage, the inductor/transformer-proxy 'hidden, under-owned' premise this residual-alpha page rewards keeps eroding at the margin, so a small negative drift, not a rank move.
verdicts.bull: Delevan inductor/transformer proxy with $735M E-Pod data-center backlog shipping early 2027.
verdicts.neutral: Data-center wins real but well-known; 2026 guidance is deliberately conservative.
verdicts.bear: Up 52% YTD and widely covered — no longer the hidden residual-alpha name this page seeks.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-node lens already discounts RRX as more cyclical-industrial than AI. The $735M E-Pod data-center backlog is positive but pre-dates the window and is shipping-deferred to 2027, while soft guidance offsets. No new catalyst since June 9 — hold at #53.
verdicts.bull: Motors/drives/power transmission feed cooling, robotics and factory automation; sizeable data-center switchgear backlog.
verdicts.neutral: Synergy and data-center HVAC story intact, but realization is a 2027 event.
verdicts.bear: Largely a cyclical-industrial synergy story with rare-earth and tariff headwinds; AI link diluted.
